test: D6-M4 Real yields & 'digital gold' (falling 10Y TIPS real yield -> buy BTC)

data.gold_leg: DEFERRED — FRED GOLDPMGBD228NLBM bot-blocked, stooq XAU needs apikey; load-bearing thesis tested via DFII10 alone
data.causality: STRICT t-1: real-yield level/change known at close t-1 positions for return realized t-1->t; carry-forward of last business-day yield to BTC trading days (point-in-time, never uses a yield dated after t)

strategy_family: 16 configs: change timers (long when L-day real-yield change<0, L in {5,10,20,40,60}, long-only & long/short) + level timers (long when yield below rolling-W median, W in {60,120,250}, LO & LS). 4bps/flip net cost.

best_config.note: beats B&H 0.473 gross but only because it is long ~half the time on a trending asset

raw_signal: corr(real-yield change, next-day BTC return) in [-0.024, +0.006] across all lookbacks — essentially noise; confirms prior 'weak/unstable correlation'

ar1_matched_placebo_p.note: the coincident-beta killer: fake AR(1) yield paths time BTC ~10% as well as the real series

beta_neutralization.note: barely significant, does not survive placebo -> lift is risk-on beta not real-yield edge

regime_split_holdout.note: OOS collapse: negatively predictive out of regime

long_block_bootstrap_mean_bps_day.note: CI excludes 0 but this is the only non-failing diagnostic and reflects beta, not alpha

binding_gate: ar1_matched_placebo (coincident-beta) + regime-split holdout OOS collapse + DSR@N
conclusion: The 'digital gold' / inflation-hedge timing thesis fails on free FRED DFII10 over 2017-2026. Raw predictive correlation is ~0. The best timer's apparent edge is risk-on beta (long ~half the time on a bull-trending asset): it does NOT beat an AR(1)-matched persistent-series placebo (p=0.096), does NOT survive pair-shuffle (p=0.120), is overfit (PBO=0.73), and inverts out-of-regime (IS +1.34 -> OOS -0.77). Confirms the documented prior KILL (inflation-hedge failed OOS in 2021-22).
